name: Grammar: Articles, Subject-Verb Agreement (Concord), and Question Tags

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - 2 dedicated TNPSC Concord MCQ sets (12 questions total)
2025_q page 146 Q194; 2022_q page 23 Q86, page 25 Q94-95; 2025_q page 137 Q166 (real, with answer key marks)

researchNotes: Eleventh and truly final Grammar topic — a round-up combining several small evidence clusters stashed across Days 74-77 that didn't fit neatly into earlier topics. Subject-verb agreement evidence clusters around 'notional singular' subjects that LOOK plural/compound but take a singular verb (collective nouns, amounts of money/time treated as a single unit, 'mathematics'-type subjects, 'each/neither/one of' constructions) — this is the single most-tested pattern in the real evidence. With this, Part C Unit I (Grammar) is genuinely exhausted at 11 topics.

reading.intro: The dominant real-exam pattern here: many subjects LOOK plural or compound but are grammatically treated as singular ('Mathematics is...', 'Ten dollars is...', 'Each of the boys was...', 'Neither of the books is...') — don't just count nouns, check the actual grammatical rule.

qa.question: Real exam: "The list of items ___ on the desk." Which verb form is correct, and why?
qa.answer: Is — the subject is 'The list' (singular), not 'items' (the noun closest to the verb); 'of items' is just a modifying phrase.

qa.question: Real exam: "Either Ram or his brothers ___ coming today." Which verb form is correct, and why?
qa.answer: Are — with 'either...or'/'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the subject CLOSEST to it ('his brothers', plural), not the first-named subject.

qa.question: Real exam: "Everyone ___ ready for the exam" and "One of my friends ___ a pilot." Which verb forms are correct?
qa.answer: Everyone IS ready (indefinite pronouns like everyone/everybody/everything are always singular). One of my friends IS a pilot ('one' is the true subject, not 'friends').

qa.question: Real exam: "Mathematics ___ a difficult subject" and "Ten dollars ___ not enough." Which verb forms are correct, and why?
qa.answer: Mathematics IS a difficult subject (subjects ending in '-ics' naming a single field of study are singular, despite looking plural). Ten dollars IS not enough (a sum of money/time/distance treated as ONE single amount takes a singular verb).

qa.question: Real exam: "Neither the teacher nor the students ___ present." Which verb form is correct?
qa.answer: Were — again, the verb agrees with the NEARER subject ('the students', plural), not 'the teacher' (singular).

qa.question: Real exam: "The quality of the apples ___ not good" and "Neither of the books ___ interesting." Which verb forms are correct?
qa.answer: The quality IS not good (subject is 'quality', singular — 'of the apples' is just a modifying phrase). Neither of the books IS interesting ('neither'/'either' are always singular, regardless of what follows 'of').

qa.question: Real exam: "Each of the boys ___ given a gift." Which verb form is correct?
qa.answer: Was — 'each' (like 'every') is always singular, regardless of the plural noun that follows ('of the boys').

qa.question: Real exam: which sentence has an error in subject-verb agreement — 'The team has been working hard' / 'Time and tide waits for no man' / 'She don't have a pen' / 'He doesn't come outside nowadays'?
qa.answer: 'She don't have a pen' is wrong — it should be 'She DOESN'T have a pen' (third-person singular subject 'she' requires 'doesn't', not 'don't').

qa.question: Real exam: fill the blank with the correct article: "I saw ___ one rupee coin lying on the ground."
qa.answer: A (not 'an') — because 'one' starts with a consonant SOUND (/w/), the same sound-based rule as 'a university'/'an hour'. Articles follow the SOUND of the next word, not its spelling.

qa.question: Real exam: why should "He is a MLA" be corrected to "He is an MLA"?
qa.answer: Because 'M' is pronounced 'em' — a vowel SOUND — even though 'M' is a consonant letter. Same sound-based rule as the previous question, applied in the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: "The list of items ___ on the desk."
"Either Ram or his brothers ___ coming today."
questions.explanation: are (agrees with the nearer subject 'his brothers').

questions.prompt: "Neither the teacher nor the students ___ present."
questions.explanation: were (agrees with the nearer subject 'the students').

questions.prompt: "Each of the boys ___ given a gift."
Which sentence has an error in subject-verb agreement?
options: The team has been working hard.
Time and tide waits for no man.
questions.explanation: She don't have a pen (should be 'doesn't').

questions.prompt: "I saw ___ one rupee coin lying on the ground."
Which is correct: "He is a MLA" or "He is an MLA"? Why?
options: 'a MLA' — M is a consonant letter
'an MLA' — M is pronounced 'em', a vowel sound
questions.explanation: 'an MLA' — M is pronounced 'em', a vowel sound.
